excludedSurfaces.app-partner: No partner-owned address or geofence mutation is part of JRN-006.
excludedSurfaces.app-captain: Captain navigation consumes later delivery-location journeys and does not own client address truth.
excludedSurfaces.app-field: Field onboarding and assignments do not own client delivery-address truth.
excludedSurfaces.webapp: No separate governed client-address runtime is registered.
excludedSurfaces.website: Public website does not process authenticated delivery addresses.

capabilities: Search input and provider results are normalized and malformed results fail closed.
Reverse geocoding validates coordinates and results before DSH service-area resolution.
Operators can read map-provider health with timeout, unavailable, not-configured, and uncertain-result states separated.
Operators can list and read one service area with polygon coordinates, point count, bounds, state, priority, and version.
Service-area writes are versioned, idempotent, audited, and reject zero-area, duplicate-edge, and self-intersecting polygons in Go and PostgreSQL.
Client address create and update require coordinates resolving to the supplied active geofence.
Deleted address PII is scheduled by versioned retention policy and queue counts plus next purge time are visible.
Due address anonymization uses one stable run identity for idempotency and correlation and is retry-safe with SKIP LOCKED.
Privacy audit readback comes from a database projection that structurally excludes raw client and address PII.
negativeInvariants: No client surface calls a map provider directly.
No malformed or incomplete provider result becomes operational truth.
No client-supplied serviceAreaCode is accepted without active geofence resolution.
No active address write succeeds without latitude and longitude.
No invalid polygon topology reaches service-area truth.
No privacy policy mutation bypasses version and idempotency checks.
No anonymization retry creates a second run result.
No raw client_id, recipient name, phone, address text, delivery instructions, or coordinates appear in privacy audit responses.
No cross-actor address PII access is permitted.
No runtime mock or fallback may establish map, address, service-area, or privacy truth.
acceptanceConditions: All eight entries in the JRN-006 slice registry are verified on the exact commit.
Targeted frontend TypeScript compilation passes for the JRN-006 control-panel and shared-brain files.
JRN-006 Go ownership packages compile and pass on the exact commit.
PostgreSQL 16 applies migrations dsh-056, 076, 078, 081, 082, 083, 906, 907, and 908 and passes database invariant tests 906, 907, and 908.
Evidence records the exact commit, workflow run, artifact id, and artifact digest before status changes to CLOSED_WITH_EVIDENCE.
